115136.explanation: Residents must really have influence, but a referendum is not always the best tool. We choose conversations in the neighborhood, clear choices, and honestly explaining what is and isn’t possible. No pseudo-participation, but real involvement that works for Harderwijk.

115139.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be given space to determine their opening hours. In Harderwijk, we work together with entrepreneurs, residents, and for example the night mayor to make agreements that fit a lively city center and a livable environment. Not everyone for themselves, but freedom in unity.

115144.explanation: A fireworks show can unite, but is not automatic. We look at what fits Harderwijk, what is safe, and what has support. Sometimes another moment or a different type of event is a better choice.

115145.explanation: Bulky waste collection costs money. We believe polluters should pay, not everyone collectively. This keeps it fair and affordable. The municipality provides good facilities and clear rules so waste is properly disposed of.

115146.explanation: Events bring life to the city, and we are proud of that. At the same time, living conditions must remain pleasant. We choose clear agreements, tailored solutions, and good communication. No extra rules for the sake of rules, but a balance between celebration and peace.

115149.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is important for information and connection in Harderwijk. At the same time, we handle public funds carefully. We assess what is needed for the broadcaster to function well and make clear agreements about that.

115151.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for meeting and health in Harderwijk. If they get into trouble, we look together at what is needed. No automatic support, but thinking along and stepping in when really necessary to keep the club afloat.

115153.explanation: Arming enforcement officers (boa’s) is not a local political choice but national policy. We focus on good training, clear task delineation, and intensive cooperation with the police. Safety requires professionalism and good preparation, not symbolic measures.

115155.explanation: Arming enforcement officers (boa’s) is not a local political choice but national policy. We focus on good training, clear task delineation, and intensive cooperation with the police. Safety requires professionalism and good preparation, not symbolic measures.

115159.explanation: Vacancy is a shame, especially with housing shortage. We encourage converting offices into homes and can help through the housing fund or alternative destinations. Not every location is suitable, so we consider what works for Harderwijk per building.

115160.explanation: Participation is important, but social assistance is not a punishment. In Harderwijk, we help people through meaningful daily activities, development, and guidance, such as through Zorgdat and Harderwijk voor Elkaar. What someone does must suit what he or she can handle.

115161.explanation: People with a low income must be able to keep participating. Waivers prevent debt and unnecessary stress. That fits with our principle that help should be simple and humane, so Harderwijk residents do not get into further trouble.

115162.explanation: We have the poverty schemes in Harderwijk well organized. A higher income threshold for everyone costs a lot and does not always help. However, we do want room for tailor-made solutions, for example via the prevention fund, for people who would otherwise fall between the cracks.

115164.explanation: More charging spots are fine, but designating extra exclusive parking spaces must be proportional to the need. Parking is scarce and should remain fair, also for people with a smaller budget. We choose tailor-made solutions per neighborhood.

115165.explanation: Traffic safety comes first. In residential areas and near schools, 30 km/h is sometimes still too fast. But not every road is the same. We opt for safe design, lower speeds where necessary, and enforcement that works in Harderwijk.

115166.explanation: Parking nuisance varies per neighborhood. We only introduce parking permits where it is really necessary. No standard measure for the entire city, but tailor-made per area, with attention to livability and accessibility in Harderwijk.

115168.explanation: Good care only works if people feel understood. That is why we find it important that care fits someone's background and beliefs. This way, help remains accessible, humane, and effective, as we envision in Harderwijk.

115170.explanation: Harderwijk already has a relatively low property tax (ozb). We want to keep it that way and handle it carefully. Local taxes must be balanced with good facilities and a healthy budget. We choose clarity and responsible policy.

115171.explanation: The dog tax is not an end in itself to abolish or increase. We look at what is fair and what is needed for facilities such as off-leash areas and enforcement. Costs and revenues must remain balanced for Harderwijk.

115172.explanation: The tourist tax was recently increased. In our program, we choose tourism balanced with the city. Visitors contribute, but it must remain fair and not go at the expense of entrepreneurs, livability, and Harderwijk’s character.

115173.explanation: Harderwijk currently has a legal task to house status holders. Once this legal obligation ends, we believe status holders should no longer receive priority in housing allocation.

115175.explanation: We are cautious about solar panels on agricultural land. Food, landscape, and nature are important. Where possible, we first choose roofs, facades, and smart combinations. No ban, but strict consideration per location.

115176.explanation: Culture is important, but now we choose focus. With Stad als Podium, we invest in better use of existing locations and a good audience experience. First make work of what is there before starting new big plans.

115178.explanation: Affordable housing is under pressure. That is why in the housing deal we agreed that at least 30% of new construction is social rental. This ensures starters and people with lower incomes keep a place in Harderwijk.

115181.explanation: We address nuisance, but relocating it is not an end in itself. We look at what works for safety, livability, and enforcement in the city center. We do this carefully and in consultation with residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services.

115182.explanation: During the busy summer months, the boulevard should primarily be a place to walk and linger. To maintain safety and space, we restrict cycling where it becomes too crowded. We do this in consultation with entrepreneurs and with clear rules.

115183.explanation: Research shows that locks on waste bins have little effect and are costly. We focus on a simple system: you place your bin at the curb yourself, and where necessary, we enforce the rules. This keeps it affordable and manageable for Harderwijk.

115184.explanation: Free short-term parking does not fit with a city center where we want to encourage staying. There is plenty to experience and parking in Harderwijk is not expensive. For quick errands, the bicycle is a logical and easy alternative for many residents.

115136.explanation: Council members are the elected representatives of the people. That is the foundation of our local democracy. They are open to residents' opinions. The municipal council must then weigh the larger interest.

115139.explanation: Clear rules regarding opening hours are important to guarantee public order and safety.

115144.explanation: We would prefer the municipality to organize a beautiful drone, laser, or LED show.

115145.explanation: Nothing is free. Those who want to have bulky waste collected must pay for it. Otherwise, the fixed fee must be raised for everyone. We do want to move to pay-per-kilo for bulky waste drop-off as soon as possible.

115146.explanation: The current standards are fine. If there are many complaints, it should be considered whether adjustments are necessary.

115149.explanation: The importance of independent local media is great. The municipality already supports this with subsidies.

115151.explanation: Associations belong to their members. The municipality already supports sports clubs in many ways. Additional subsidies are only given in exceptional situations.

115153.explanation: Safety is important. Tackling nuisance must be a priority. But that does not necessarily mean more enforcement officers; sometimes street coaches or neighborhood workers are more effective. Approaches should be tailored per neighborhood, together with residents.

115155.explanation: At this time, there is no reason to introduce this in Harderwijk. It can lead to more aggression instead of de-escalation.

115159.explanation: The municipality must facilitate this by adjusting the zoning plan. The owner must pay for it since they also benefit from the increase in value.

115160.explanation: Participation is important for returning to the labor market. Staying active is good for health.

115161.explanation: Debt forgiveness only happens in special situations, and stopping it could push people further into debt. That would be counterproductive.

115162.explanation: In Harderwijk, we already use a more generous income limit than is customary nationally.

115164.explanation: The municipality monitors the need for charging stations. Everyone who buys an electric car can request a nearby charging station.

115165.explanation: This should happen on unsafe roads and in residential areas, and more residential zones can be created where 15 km/h is the standard. Connecting roads should remain 50 km/h to maintain traffic flow. Use temporary speed-reducing measures where permanent adjustment is not yet possible.

115166.explanation: Implement paid parking in residential neighborhoods only if there are structural parking problems and only after consultation with residents.

115168.explanation: People should have the choice to select an appropriate healthcare provider if that is reasonably possible.

115170.explanation: We want to keep facilities like the swimming pool or the library open. This is not possible if the municipality's income decreases. Therefore, the property tax is necessary.

115171.explanation: The dog tax is used for dog-related facilities. The nuisance from dog mess has not decreased enough to abolish the tax.

115172.explanation: The tourist tax has already been increased in recent years due to budget cuts.

115173.explanation: The municipality has a legal obligation from the national government to provide housing for refugees with a residence permit (status holders). The only way to do this is to give this relatively small group urgency.

115175.explanation: We are cautious about solar panels on agricultural land because there are still enough roofs available for solar panel installation.

115176.explanation: This is financially a utopia. We favor a realistic approach: an upgrade of an existing location.

115178.explanation: Build neighborhoods where people live together and create housing in all price categories: 30% social rental, 10% middle rent, 30% affordable owner-occupied, and 20% expensive homes. This means at least two-thirds of new constructions fall into the affordable segment.

115181.explanation: A study was conducted into an alternative location for the coffee shop. The alternatives carry greater risks than maintaining the current location. Preferably, we do not want a coffee shop in Harderwijk.

115182.explanation: If everyone is a bit considerate, it can work as it is now. Temporary markings on the road are an option. For cyclists, the boulevard is an important connecting route. However, we want to get rid of cars and parking on the boulevard.

115183.explanation: Research in other municipalities shows there is no necessity. If residents still want this, they can pay for it themselves. The amount is manageable.

115184.explanation: If the first two hours of parking become free again, municipal taxes must go up. It will lead to lower parking revenues and therefore less money for other services.

115136.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

115139.explanation: The municipality should not be patronizing here. Cafés and restaurants may make their own choices. This way, residents are served as well as possible.

115144.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

115145.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

115146.explanation: We support a limit that approaches the World Health Organization's advice to protect hearing and limit nuisance, but believe this should be done in consultation with various parties given the importance of festivals for the city's vibrancy.

115149.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to keep afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

115151.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

115153.explanation: Enforcement officers in Harderwijk are overburdened, making it difficult to sufficiently tackle nuisance in neighborhoods.

115155.explanation: Enforcement officers are not police and do not have the same training or role. Equipping enforcement officers with more weapons risks budget cuts for the police.

115159.explanation: In many places in Harderwijk, such as the ground floor of the Old Library and the Poets' Quarter, offices have been empty for a long time. There is a great need for housing, so the municipality must enable this.

115160.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

115161.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

115162.explanation: There is still much hidden poverty in Harderwijk. The municipality can help with this.

115164.explanation: More charging stations are needed, but making parking spaces available only to electric cars causes parking problems.

115165.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

115166.explanation: First, the municipality must ensure that public transport is good enough.

115168.explanation: This way, healthcare better aligns with residents' needs. Of course, this is not possible for all types of care. But if the service is available, people should be able to choose it.

115170.explanation: We do not opt for easy tax benefits for homeowners. It is ultimately the tenants who pay for this if the amenities are the same.

115171.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA has wanted to abolish this tax for years, but most parties are opposed. It is an unfair tax, where you have to pay for one pet but not for another.

115172.explanation: The tourist tax should be proportional to the costs Harderwijk incurs. Tourists are also expected to contribute to our beautiful city.

115173.explanation: There are many groups with urgent needs: divorced parents, young people, status holders, and many more. The municipality must find a balance between these groups.

115175.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

115176.explanation: A theater on the edge of the city center is naturally the best location. The College keeps postponing decisions about a new theater, while a city like Harderwijk deserves a beautiful, easily accessible theater as close to the city as possible.

115178.explanation: Actually, 30% is still too little. In new neighborhoods like Waterfront and Harderweide, there has already been too little attention to this, causing waiting lists to grow.

115181.explanation: Extensive searches for other locations have been done in recent years, but none have been found. If a good alternative location becomes available, we are not against relocation.

115182.explanation: Safety must be properly considered, but the boulevard is also an important cycling route for residents and tourists.

115183.explanation: Paying per kilo means you pay for what your neighbor puts in your container. It is incomprehensible that the municipality wants to shift this problem onto the residents.

115184.explanation: Parking rates in Harderwijk are not high, and the parking garage is always full on peak days. It is strange that cycling residents have to pay more for parking facilities used by visitors with cars.

115136.explanation: The VVD Harderwijk - Hierden chooses citizen participation. Careful participation. Residents must be involved early and honestly. Participation should not be a checkbox but a serious opportunity to have a say. We want to weigh the voice of young people through a youth council.

115139.explanation: The VVD wants to give entrepreneurs every opportunity to do business as they wish.

115144.explanation: With the upcoming fireworks ban, we see this as a good solution to enjoy fireworks responsibly.

115145.explanation: We advocate for two free bulky waste disposals per year. For residents who are not mobile, free home pickup is a good alternative.

115146.explanation: The VVD favors prevention. By lowering noise limits, festival visitors are less likely to suffer hearing damage, which in the long run reduces pressure on ENT specialists.

115149.explanation: It is important that (local) journalism is accessible to our residents. We therefore support local journalism but remain critical regarding independent revenue generation through advertising, etc.

115151.explanation: The VVD chooses to keep the financial pressure on sports clubs low.

115153.explanation: Safety is important to the VVD. BOA officers are an important link when it comes to safety. It is important they are equipped with the right tools to act appropriately.

115155.explanation: Like other emergency workers, BOAs increasingly face aggression. The VVD sees a baton as a good tool for BOAs to defend themselves.

115159.explanation: The real solution lies in relaxing unnecessary rules and procedures that delay construction. Converting offices and other vacant buildings into housing can help. Subsidies should never be automatic and must be considered case by case for efficiency.

115160.explanation: The principle is that everyone contributes according to their ability. For those who cannot (any longer) work, options are considered, such as a customized workplace or meaningful daily activities if someone has a disability. Staying actively involved in society is good for everyone. Those who can work but do not want to may have their benefits reduced.

115161.explanation: The VVD stands for low municipal taxes for everyone. Everyone who can contributes according to their means to societal provisions that cost money. For exceptional cases, we opt for tailored solutions via existing schemes.

115162.explanation: However, there is nuance: No child should be left out. We support, among other things, financially enabling children to do sports, but also music lessons, swimming lessons, or school fees if their parents cannot afford this. Those moving from benefits to work must always be financially better off.

115164.explanation: Depends on current needs per neighborhood. Optionally, flexible parking spaces can also be considered.

115165.explanation: When setting a speed limit, the design of the specific road must be considered. For example, in Harderwijk the Groene Zoomweg was made for 60 km/h, while currently 50 km/h is allowed there.

115166.explanation: As a party, we stand for realistic parking standards and not those set according to the Randstad. Introducing parking permits creates a waterbed effect, where the circle of parking permits keeps expanding.

115168.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but this freedom should not cause healthcare costs to rise faster or make care less accessible. It is primarily important that our care is of high quality and accessible to everyone regardless of religion, culture, or belief.

115170.explanation: In recent years, the burdens have increased considerably, and homeowners are also facing large investments, for example in sustainability. Therefore, we will ensure these burdens remain as low as possible for both residents and entrepreneurs.

115171.explanation: We will abolish old-fashioned taxes such as the dog tax, unless this amount fully benefits well-maintained facilities for dogs.

115172.explanation: The tourist tax remains as low as possible and the income is used to improve the quality of recreation and tourism.

115173.explanation: Housing new residents. We take our responsibility for housing guest workers and status holders, but they have no separate priority position compared to other target groups within our municipality.

115175.explanation: Only if no other option remains, then on agricultural land. But the preference is first on roofs, parking lots, etc.

115176.explanation: Only if there is a commercial operator for this.

115178.explanation: Building should be based on demand in the housing market. Flexibility is required in this.

115181.explanation: The VVD advocates preserving the coffeeshop. This way, the sale of soft drugs can be regulated. If the coffeeshop were to disappear, there is a risk that trade would shift to illegal street sales.

115182.explanation: Our boulevard is an important part of a popular cycling route. We support the well-known 'slow zones' and appeal to the traffic sense of cyclists.

115183.explanation: These bins should be charged through waste costs. The bins are unnecessary because people only put out their bin when it is full. We advocate proven effective measures to control costs, pay-per-kilo pricing for the gray bin, and maintaining twice-yearly free bulky waste disposal.

115184.explanation: The VVD advocates the return of two hours of free parking in the city center to strengthen accessibility and convenience. This keeps the city center attractive for both residents and visitors, and prevents shoppers from going to neighboring municipalities.

115136.explanation: Our representatives sit in the municipal council and the executive board. However, we value the involvement of our residents. Therefore, we are organizing a pilot citizen's assembly to involve our residents even better.

115139.explanation: It is important to us that entrepreneurs jointly determine desirable opening hours. This enhances the attractiveness of our municipality.

115144.explanation: A fireworks show can be a beautiful alternative to the fireworks ban and provide a unifying event during New Year's Eve.

115145.explanation: Nothing is free when it comes to waste. Ultimately, all costs related to waste are included in the waste disposal levy.

115146.explanation: Events must comply with laws and regulations. Noise standards are set nationally.

115149.explanation: We recognize the importance of local broadcasters.

115151.explanation: We see the importance of sports and exercise. Clubs in difficult situations will receive financial support from us.

115153.explanation: We see that there is more need for this here, for example in industrial areas and in cases of nuisance.

115155.explanation: As CDA, we have requested this before and find it important that BOAs (special enforcement officers) themselves indicate what they consider desirable.

115159.explanation: We prefer to leave this to the market.

115161.explanation: These people already struggle to make ends meet, and we make it even harder for them to get by. However, we want to focus on self-reliance so that residents can get out of this difficult situation.

115162.explanation: Our income limit is already set at 120% of the subsistence minimum.

115164.explanation: If demand increases, we will have to designate more places for this.

115165.explanation: Traffic flow in our municipality is important. All roads having a 30 km/h limit doesn’t always contribute to this. However, we do want to create more bicycle streets where cars are guests.

115166.explanation: If necessary to reduce parking pressure, this can be helpful.

115168.explanation: Due to labor market shortages, it is already difficult to find enough people at the moment. Therefore, we cannot afford to set conditions in this regard.

115170.explanation: Any increase may not exceed the standard trend.

115171.explanation: This can only be done if we also abolish the facilities for it.

115172.explanation: This was recently increased by 25%. This is sufficient and makes us competitive in the area.

115173.explanation: As municipalities, we have no authority over this. We adhere to national laws and regulations.

115175.explanation: Solar panels should first be placed on roofs and only as a last resort on agricultural land.

115176.explanation: If the opportunity arises, we are open to it. We will not actively pursue it with farmers.

115178.explanation: We find it important that there is housing for all income groups.

115181.explanation: If there is a good alternative for this.

115182.explanation: This strengthens the attractiveness of the boulevard.

115183.explanation: National figures show that ditches are not necessary to prevent waste dumping.

115184.explanation: We find it more important that there are enough parking spaces. We want to look into lowering parking fees during the low season.

115136.explanation: Voting on a subject that concerns all residents of Harderwijk and Hierden is best decided by a vote. Over four years, files and residents’ opinions on issues may have changed.

115139.explanation: Complete freedom of opening hours goes too far, but rules that are too strict do as well. Municipal Interests want to apply tailor-made solutions by extending hours in, for example, the entertainment district, while maintaining peace in residential areas.

115144.explanation: New Year's Eve belongs to and is for the residents themselves. A mandatory municipal fireworks show costs a lot of money and does not resolve nuisance. It is better to focus on enforcement, safety, and responsibility from individuals themselves.

115145.explanation: Free bulky waste collection prevents litter and dumping. That is better for livability and ultimately cheaper than cleaning up afterward. Organize it smartly and prevent abuse. Think realistically about waste collection.

115146.explanation: Events are part of a lively city. Protect residents with clear agreements and enforcement, but don’t make it impossible with ever stricter rules. Balance livability and enjoyment.

115149.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is valuable, but extra funding should be tied to quality, reach, and independence. First performance, then support.

115151.explanation: Sports clubs are an important part of our community. Temporary and targeted support may be necessary to keep them. The municipality can temporarily support clubs financially, but they remain responsible for their own existence.

115153.explanation: More visible enforcement increases safety and livability. BOAs are not a luxury but a necessity, especially in cases of nuisance and minor crime. Address problems early, in the interest of residents.

115155.explanation: BOAs working on safety must be able to protect themselves. A baton increases their resilience and authority, provided they are well trained and used responsibly. Safety starts with those who enforce.

115159.explanation: Transformation can help with housing shortages, but subsidies are not always necessary. Evaluate feasibility and desirability per location. The municipality should only intervene where things stall and it is necessary.

115160.explanation: Those who can participate should participate. Socially meaningful work provides structure, self-worth, and contributes to society. With attention to customization for those who truly cannot.

115161.explanation: Waiving payment should not be automatic. Aid must be targeted at those who truly cannot pay and need temporary help. Personal responsibility remains the principle, with customization where necessary.

115162.explanation: Aid must reach those who truly need it. A higher threshold can sometimes be logical, but prevent situations where working pays off less. Focus on customization and local costs, not on generic expansion.

115164.explanation: Charging points are necessary, but parking pressure is already high. Expand only where it is truly needed and maintain a balance with parking needs for all residents.

115165.explanation: 30 km/h can improve safety in some places, but not everywhere. Apply it selectively where needed. No general measure that harms traffic flow and accessibility.

115166.explanation: Parking permits can help in areas with high parking pressure, but they are not an end in themselves. Implement them only where truly necessary and involve residents in the decision-making.

115168.explanation: Care must be appropriate and as much as possible align with faith, culture, and convictions. Quality of care must be the basis for our residents. The municipality is responsible for good and suitable care for every individual within the set financial frameworks.

115170.explanation: The property tax (ozb) burdens families and entrepreneurs. So if the ozb can be lowered, that is desirable. But the municipality must handle money frugally and reduce burdens where possible. So do not automatically pass the costs on to residents.

115171.explanation: The municipality uses this income to clean up dog waste.

115172.explanation: Tourism is important for Harderwijk and our entrepreneurs. A higher tourist tax makes the city less attractive. Keep Harderwijk hospitable and competitive, without extra burdens on visitors and businesses.

115173.explanation: Scarce social rental homes should primarily go to local residents who have been waiting for years. Integration starts with fair policy, not with priority.

115175.explanation: Agricultural land is intended for food production, not for large-scale solar parks. Put solar panels on roofs and business parks. Protect the countryside and choose smart spatial use.

115176.explanation: A theater can add something, but only if it is financially feasible and not a prestige project. It must not become a municipal project. First use existing facilities well and fairly weigh costs and risks.

115178.explanation: Social rental housing is important, but a fixed percentage does not work everywhere. Look by location at needs and flow-through. A mix of social, affordable, and owner-occupied is needed to keep Harderwijk livable.

115181.explanation: The coffeeshop is in a good location and has its organization well arranged. In the recent extension of the location, pros and cons were weighed, and it turned out there is no better spot than the current one. That is a sober and wise choice.

115182.explanation: Safety on the boulevard is important, but a complete ban is too rigid. Choose clear rules, enforcement, and time slots where it is really busy, so recreation and accessibility remain balanced.

115183.explanation: If you pay per kilo, misuse must be prevented. Residents should not pay for others’ waste. Locks on bins are expensive. The municipality can create a budget to contribute to residents who want to purchase a lock themselves. That is good policy.

115184.explanation: Free parking for the first hours makes the city center more accessible to residents and visitors and helps entrepreneurs; this stimulates liveliness. Several studies show that residents shop elsewhere faster if they can park for free there.

115136.explanation: D66 wants Harderwijk residents to be able to participate in important decisions. That is why we support an advisory referendum. This way residents can express their opinion on municipal council decisions, with a clear question and good explanation. That strengthens our local democracy.

115139.explanation: D66 wants cafes and restaurants to determine their own opening hours. Entrepreneurs know best what suits their establishment and neighborhood. By giving them this freedom, we encourage entrepreneurship and liveliness in Harderwijk, within clear and fair rules.

115144.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to organize a central fireworks show on New Year's Eve. As a pilot, together with residents and as environmentally friendly as possible. This offers a safe and festive alternative to a total fireworks ban, celebrating the new year together.

115145.explanation: D66 Harderwijk wants to enable residents to bring bulky waste free of charge to the recycling center twice a year. Free collection of bulky waste from all residents involves enormous costs. That is unfair to people who do not use it but still have higher costs.

115146.explanation: D66 believes the noise standards at festivals and parties are strict enough. We trust organizers to follow the rules. Events provide atmosphere and meeting opportunities and make the city lively. Harderwijk should remain a vibrant and lively city.

115149.explanation: D66 wants to additionally support the local broadcaster. Strong local journalism is important for a well-informed city. Especially in times of fake news the local broadcaster is essential here. D66 wants to deploy fact-checks in politics, in cooperation with local media.

115151.explanation: D66 wants to support sports clubs when they face financial difficulties. Clubs are important for Harderwijk and Hierden and ensure that people can continue to exercise and meet each other. That is good for health, prevention, and a strong, connected city.

115153.explanation: D66 wants more municipal enforcement officers in Harderwijk. BOAs provide visibility on the street and are approachable for residents. With more enforcers we increase safety, prevent nuisance, and ensure a pleasant and safe living environment in neighborhoods.

115155.explanation: This is not the municipality's responsibility. Only the police may use force in the Netherlands. BOAs are important supervisors and connectors. The safety of BOAs is paramount and first requires good support: sufficient staffing, clear tasks, cooperation with police, and prevention.

115159.explanation: D66 wants it to become easier to prevent vacancies and convert buildings into homes. This can be through subsidies but also by relaxing rules. This way we build additional homes faster, tackle vacancies, and help starters and home seekers find a suitable place.

115160.explanation: D66 Harderwijk chooses participation with trust and customization. Those on social assistance can do socially meaningful work if it fits: it helps to stay active and maintain perspective on work. Always attentive to health and personal circumstances.

115161.explanation: D66 wants everyone to be able to participate. Waiving municipal taxes for residents with a low income, including the self-employed, prevents financial stress, debts, and health problems. This way we ensure people can make ends meet, keep Harderwijk social and fair, and leave no one behind.

115162.explanation: D66 wants to help people who need assistance. Just above the income threshold problems can arise because people then do not qualify for schemes. D66 wants to help these people with smart arrangements, early detection, and problem prevention.

115164.explanation: D66 wants to designate more parking spaces as charging spots for electric cars. That makes electric driving easier for more residents. We also want to make home charging simpler, for example with cable ducts. This way we accelerate the transition to cleaner transport in Harderwijk.

115165.explanation: D66 wants the maximum speed within built-up areas to be 30 km/h. That makes streets safer and more manageable. Children must be able to cycle and walk to school safely. With 30 km/h we reduce accidents and make Harderwijk safer for everyone.

115166.explanation: D66 believes parking permits are not an end goal. We look per neighborhood at what is needed to regulate parking well. Sometimes a permit can help, but often other solutions are better to keep neighborhoods livable and accessible.

115168.explanation: D66 wants people who need care always to be able to choose a care provider that matches their faith, culture, or belief. Good care fits who you are. Freedom of choice increases trust and makes people feel safe and taken seriously.

115170.explanation: D66 supports good facilities. Lowering property tax (OZB) sounds attractive, but means less money for care, safety, education, and facilities in Harderwijk. We choose stable housing costs and a healthy budget, so the municipality can keep investing in what residents need daily.

115171.explanation: D66 wants the dog tax to be used properly. This tax ensures dog owners contribute to maintenance and good dog-walking areas. Abolishing it means that costs fall on everyone. That is not fair to residents without dogs.

115172.explanation: D66 believes that the tourist tax can be increased. Harderwijk is an attractive city with good facilities that visitors enjoy. A slightly higher contribution is fair and still remains lower than in many other cities. This way we invest together in a beautiful city.

115173.explanation: D66 believes that people who fled war and violence need our help. Refugees have a legal right to a social rental home. Harderwijk implements the national law carefully, providing clarity and fair distribution of social homes for everyone.

115175.explanation: Solar panels provide green energy and are better for the climate. We follow the solar ladder: first roofs and sites, and only then ground. There must be room for sustainable choices. Land for housing always takes priority, but a total ban is not necessary.

115176.explanation: D66 supports good stage facilities. That is why it is wiser to fully invest now in improving current locations than to choose new construction. New construction entails high costs. Unfortunately, that is not sensible in the current financial situation.

115178.explanation: D66 wants at least 30% social rental housing in new construction in Harderwijk. This aligns with national agreements and legal obligations. By building enough social rental housing, we ensure homes for all incomes and build a balanced city.

115181.explanation: D66 wants the coffeeshop in the city center to remain. This coffeeshop provides education, referral in case of problems, and invests in Harderwijk charities. Additionally, there is a lot of supervision in the city center. Things are going well now and it does not cause extra insecurity.

115182.explanation: D66 does not want to ban cycling on the boulevard. The boulevard belongs to everyone. Instead of a ban, we choose better design, with clear routes and more space. This ensures safety and keeps the boulevard accessible for cyclists and pedestrians.

115183.explanation: D66 trusts the residents. Locks are expensive and cause everyone to pay more. Therefore, D66 is against locks on containers. Whoever places their full container by the road prevents others from adding waste.

115184.explanation: D66 supports promoting sustainable transport, such as cycling or public transport. Free parking does not contribute to this. At the same time, we have good parking facilities that require maintenance. We ask users to contribute to these costs.

statements.title: The cannabis coffeeshop should be removed from the city centre.

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Residents of Harderwijk should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Harderwijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Harderwijk should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The first two hours of parking in the city centre should be free again.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Harderwijk should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Harderwijk controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Harderwijk must introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Harderwijk moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Harderwijk must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Harderwijk moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must ban bicycles on the boulevard from May to September.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be a new theater on the waterfront.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality must provide locks for the garbage bins (wheelie bins) if pay-by-weight waste is introduced.
